channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: 3 patients are being evacuated to Europe from ship with hantavirus outbreak
item.description: Three patients with suspected hantavirus infections were being evacuated from a cruise ship to the Netherlands on Wednesday, the U.N. health agency said, as the vessel at the center of a deadly outbreak remained off Cape Verde with nearly 150 people on board waiting to head to Spain’s Canary Islands.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Who is Narges Mohammadi, Iranian Nobel laureate at risk of dying in custody?
item.description: Jailed Iranian Nobel Peace Prize winner Narges Mohammadi could die in prison unless she is urgently given proper health care after suffering two suspected heart attacks in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Film show: French stars set to sparkle at Cannes
item.description: We look ahead to some of the French films making a splash on the Riviera at the 79th Cannes Film Festival, as Manon Kerjean takes us through some of the home-grown talent gracing the red carpet this year. She tells us why opening feature "The Electric Kiss" strikes the right tone with its bittersweet, supernatural elements and how Arthur Harari's "The Unknown" has revisited the body-swap genre with panache.
item.dc:creator: Manon Kerjean

item.title: CNN’s pioneering founder Ted Turner dies at 87
item.description: Ted Turner, the founder of American media giant CNN, has died, the network said on Wednesday. He was 87.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: France reopens probe into Rwanda’s ex-first lady over 1994 genocide
item.description: France is reopening a probe into the alleged role Rwanda’s former first lady, 83-year-old Agathe Habyarimana, played in the 1994 genocide in which around 800,000 people, mainly ethnic Tutsis, were killed. Last year, the case against the late ex-president’s wife was dismissed due to "insufficient evidence", but on Wednesday a French appeals court overturned the ruling.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Israeli court rejects appeal by detained Gaza aid flotilla activists
item.description: An Israeli court on Wednesday rejected an appeal by two foreign nationals, one Spanish and one Brazilian, challenging their detention in Israel. The two men were seized in international waters last week while participating in a humanitarian aid flotilla seeking to break Israel’s blockade of the Gaza Strip. The ruling means an earlier decision extending their detention remains in place.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Iran 'slopaganda': We tried recreating viral Lego-style AI videos
item.description: After Donald Trump announced a pause to the US operation in the Strait of Hormuz, Iran's online propaganda machine was quick to declare victory. Explosive Media, one of the groups behind Lego-style videos mocking Trump, proclaimed it "TACO Tuesday", ie that the US president had "chickened out". Meanwhile, Minecraft, the Minions and Simpsons-style characters are joining the legions of copycats. FRANCE 24's Technology Correspondent Peter O’Brien looks at how these videos are actually made.
item.dc:creator: Peter O'BRIEN

item.title: UK elections: What does it mean to be Scottish today?
item.description: As British voters head to the polls for local elections on May 7, Elena Pompei, a journalist with ENTR’s English-language team, takes us to Scotland, where she has been reporting on how young people are feeling ahead of the vote. Scottish voters will be casting ballots on Thursday to elect all 129 Members of the Scottish Parliament. Elena and her team asked two young Scots where they see the future of their country: in the UK, or independent?
item.dc:creator: François PICARD

item.title: Nearly 600,000 Venezuelans in limbo after losing temporary protected status
item.description: In 2025, the US Supreme Court provisionally authorised the Trump administration to lift Temporary Protected Status (TPS) for hundreds of thousands of Venezuelans living in the United States. This status had allowed them to work legally and remain in the country as long as their return to Venezuela was deemed dangerous. Nearly 600,000 people now live in a state of profound legal uncertainty. FRANCE 24's Fanny Allard and Fraser Jackson report from Florida.
item.dc:creator: Fanny ALLARD

item.title: War in Ukraine: Russia attacks east and south Ukraine with over 100 drones
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky said Wednesday that Russia had decided to reject efforts to halt fighting and save lives by launching fresh attacks on Ukraine, which had called a unilateral ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: AT Frenchies spread language, culture and laughs to millions
item.description: Our guests have made it their mission to share their love of the French language and culture around the world, and it is certainly working! They have over 6 million followers on social media, they've done videos with presidents and prime ministers and they're now working on their first book. Their names are Alexandre Durand and Tom Carles, but you might know them better as AT Frenchies. They spoke to us in this edition of Entre Nous.
item.dc:creator: Genie GODULA

item.title: Looking ahead to the UK's local elections with British politics expert Colm Murphy
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is facing his biggest test yet as the UK heads to the polls on Thursday for local elections. Voters in England will be charged with picking their local councillors, while in Scotland and Wales, representatives of the devolved parliaments will be elected. Polls are already predicting the ruling Labour Party will suffer its worst results in decades, with Reform UK and the Green Party expected to make gains. We look ahead to the vote with Colm Murphy, senior lecturer in British politics at Queen Mary University.
item.dc:creator: Yinka OYETADE

item.title: Hantavirus: Latest updates on the ongoing outbreak aboard the MV Hondius
item.description: A luxury cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak and marooned for days off the coast of Cape Verde ​with close to 150 people on board was due to head to Spain, while South Africa confirmed it identified among the victims a strain of the virus that can spread among humans.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Rolling Stones announce July 10 release of new album 'Foreign Tongues'
item.description: The Rolling Stones will release a new album on July 10 named "Foreign Tongues," the legendary British rockers announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: United States: Los Angeles fire suspect had grudge against wealthy
item.description: The man accused of deliberately setting a deadly fire that torched an upmarket swathe of Los Angeles last year harbored a grudge against the wealthy and admired Luigi Mangione, the man accused of shooting dead an insurance company CEO, federal prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran Nobel winner Narges Mohammadi at risk of dying in custody
item.description: Angela Diffley, France24 International Affairs Editor, has more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Arms deliveries and Africa Corps training: How Russia is bolstering its presence in Madagascar
item.description: From deliveries of drones and armoured vehicles to training sessions led by Russian Africa Corps instructors, security cooperation between Madagascar and Russia has intensified since the October 2025 coup. Russia’s growing influence is expanding into other sectors as well, raising questions within parts of Malagasy civil society.
item.source: © Russian Embassy in Madagascar / Presidency of the Refoundation of the Republic of Madagascar
item.dc:creator: The FRANCE 24 Observers

item.title: Venice Biennale: Boycotts and threats over participation of Russia & Israel
item.description: The Venice Biennale previewed its 61st and most chaotic edition ever on Tuesday, just days after the unprecedented resignation of its jury over the participation of Israel and Russia undermined the very structure of the world’s oldest contemporary art exhibition.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US suspends Hormuz operation: Washington believes close to deal with Iran
item.description: Kethevane Gorjestani gives us her analysis.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iranian and Chinese FMS meet as Beijing urges countries to maintain ceasefire
item.description: Yena Lee, France24 correspondent in Beijing China, has more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Injured Mbappe faces backlash over Sardinia trip before Clasico
item.description: Sidelined with a hamstring injury, Real Madrid striker Kylian Mbappe has sparked anger among a section of the club's supporters after a holiday in Sardinia, triggering a fresh media storm ahead of Sunday's Clasico against Barcelona.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Donald Trump renews pressure and plans to hike tariffs on EU cars
item.description: Car parts makers in Europe are ‌facing renewed pressure as Trump last week ⁠announced a tariff increase on cars and trucks from the European Union ​to 25% from the previously agreed 15%, saying the bloc had not complied with its trade deal ‌with Washington.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship heads to Spain to dock in Canary Islands
item.description: A luxury cruise ship that ​has been hit by an outbreak of the deadly hantavirus was preparing to travel from Cape Verde towards Europe on Wednesday after the Spanish government gave permission for it to dock in the Canary Islands. Sarah Morris has more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Kyiv says Russia broke unilateral ceasefire with drone and missile attacks
item.description: Kyiv said on Wednesday that Russia had violated a unilateral ceasefire called by Ukraine by attacking Ukrainian cities with 108 combat drones and three missiles overnight.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24
